positions.position: AI wrappers are dead — model providers ship features directly. The wedge is proprietary data + workflow, not API wrapping.
evidence: Anthropic shipping Remote Control, Agent Teams, MCP natively
OpenAI shipping Codex, Operator, built-in tools
Enterprise consolidating spend on proven solutions, not wrappers (Sapphire Ventures 2026)
positions.implications: Any product that's essentially wrapping Claude/GPT API is on borrowed time. Build around proprietary data or unique workflows.

positions.position: Solo founders win on context engineering + distribution, not product quality. Product quality is table stakes in 2026.
evidence: 41.8M solopreneurs in US, $1.3T economy contribution
60K+ GitHub repos with CLAUDE.md agent instructions
NxCode: 'context engineering is the era of thinking for AI, not talking to it'
Capital efficiency 10-50x vs traditional startups
positions.implications: Stop polishing product. Focus on distribution channels and context engineering depth. The product that reaches users wins over the better product that doesn't.

positions.position: The Claude Code workflow tool market is commodity. 349+ skills, 40+ orchestrators, 100+ agent collections. No moat in more agents or better prompts.
evidence: awesome-claude-code: 26.7k stars listing 40+ orchestration systems
everything-claude-code: 63k stars with 112 agents, 146 skills
Multiple 'AI OS' blueprints published (DEV Community, GitHub)
find-skills meta-skill: 418K installs — discovery itself is commoditized
positions.implications: rhino-os competing on agents/skills/hooks is a losing game. Differentiate on knowledge compounding, strategic intelligence, or taste replication.

positions.position: Knowledge compounding is the only defensible innovation in AI developer tools. Memory plugins are emerging but none do preference learning from observed behavior.
evidence: claude-mem: 33k stars, captures sessions mechanically but doesn't learn preferences
PAHF paper (Meta, Feb 2026): three-step preference learning loop from feedback
Compound engineering (Every): each session makes next session better via formalized knowledge
positions.implications: The taste/preference learning system is genuinely novel. No competitor has behavior-based preference extraction for developer workflows.

positions.position: Taste is the bottleneck when AI makes execution free. Judgment — what to build, what to cut, what to ship — is the scarce resource.
evidence: MJ Kang: 'AI makes execution cheap, making taste the primary differentiator'
HBR portfolio management: Buy/Sell/Hold scoring with kill triggers needed
Code quality is no longer a differentiator — every AI tool produces passing code
positions.implications: Build systems that capture and replicate the founder's taste/judgment. This is what scales a solo founder beyond their own attention bandwidth.

positions.position: Campus infrastructure is underserved because incumbents sell to administrators, not students. Student-first tools have a distribution advantage.
evidence: Enterprise AI solutions target admin buyers (budgets, procurement)
Students adopt tools bottom-up via word-of-mouth, no procurement cycle
UB-specific: captive audience, known pain points, zero competition for AI-native campus tools
positions.implications: HIVE's campus positioning is a real wedge if distribution is student-to-student, not admin-to-student.
